children.heading: Sexual Assault Forensic Exam Procedure.—
children.label: a Sexual Assault Forensic Exam Procedure.—
A
children.text: as soon as possible after the victim requests an examination; and

children.label: B
children.text: that is treated with the same level of urgency as emergency medical care.

children.label: A
children.text: the health, safety, and privacy of a victim of sexual assault;

children.label: B
children.text: the proximity of ashore or afloat medical facilities, including coordination as necessary with the Department of Defense, including other military departments (as defined in );section 101 of title 10

children.label: D
children.text: the operational capabilities of the vessel concerned;

children.label: E
children.text: the qualifications of medical personnel onboard;

children.label: F
children.text: coordination with law enforcement and the preservation of evidence;

children.label: G
children.text: the means of accessing a sexual assault forensic examination and medical care with a restricted report of sexual assault;

children.label: H
children.text: the availability of nonprescription pregnancy prophylactics; and

children.heading: .—Annual Report
children.label: b .—Annual Report
children.text: The Commandant shall submit to the Transportation and Infrastructure Committee of the House of Representatives and the Commerce, Science, and Transportation Committee of the Senate a report containing the number of sexual assault forensic examinations that were requested by, but not administered within 3 days to, alleged victims of sexual assault when such victims were onboard a Coast Guard vessel.
